name: Stuffed Onions
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fresh parsley or 2 teaspoons dried parsley, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven, bring a small amount of water to a boil.
2. Peel onions; using a slotted spoon, place onions in boiling water. Cook for 4-6 minutes or until softened. Remove and let stand until cool enough to handle.
3. Cut a 1/4-inch slice off the top of each onion. Remove the center, leaving a half-inch shell.
4. Chop the centers and tops of onions; set aside.
5. In a skillet, cook bacon until crisp. Remove to paper towel to drain.
6. In the drippings, sauté chopped onion until tender. Stir in bread crumbs and parsley.
7. Add butter and mushrooms; cook until mushrooms are tender.
8. Add bacon, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
9. Stuff onion shells with the mixture. Place in an ungreased shallow 1-quart baking dish.
10. Pour broth around onions.
11. Bake, uncovered, at 375 degrees for 45 minutes or until tender, basting frequently during the first 15 minutes.
12. Sprinkle with parsley if desired.
